title: GAME_PLAN.md
description: Schema for a game plan document. The Director outputs a team config that the Scaffolder uses to generate this. Each top-level property maps to a section in the markdown file.

game_name.description: The game's name. Used as the document title and in generated agent stubs.

team.description: One entry per vocabulary role. Every role must be accounted for — either active, merged, or skipped. No role can be silently omitted.

role_id.description: Must match a role id in vocab/roles/.

active.description: True if this role has a generated agent stub. False if merged or skipped.

merged_into.description: If active is false and role was merged, the role_id it was absorbed into. Omit if skipped entirely.

notes.description: Why this role is active / merged / skipped. Required when active is false — the Director must justify the decision.

phases.description: Ordered execution phases. Phase 0 is always the engine skeleton / localhost proof. QA gates are required between phases that produce playable output.

name.description: Short phase label, e.g. 'Phase 0 — Engine Skeleton'.

description.description: What gets built in this phase. Concrete deliverables, not goals.

agents.description: Role ids of agents doing work in this phase.

parallel.description: True if all agents in this phase can run simultaneously. Defaults to false.

qa_gate.description: What QA must verify before the next phase begins. Omit only for non-playable phases (e.g. art-only passes).

deferred_decisions.description: Decisions not made in this plan — explicitly owned by a specific agent. Mirrors known_unknowns from CONCEPT.md, plus any new ones the Director identifies.
